methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Growth → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.2x) to $100M total raised proxy.

data.narrative:
Ocean Infinity, a Growth-stage ocean and maritime technology company founded by Oliver Plunkett, has raised over $100M to date. The comparable universe is limited: no public peers are directly available, and only one relevant private comparable—Orbital Marine Power—could be identified within the sector.

Valuation analysis relies on a stage-progression methodology, which applies a 1.2x step-up multiplier typical of Growth-stage advancement to the $100M total-raised proxy. This approach yields an implied valuation of $120M. The constrained dataset and single-method triangulation produce a valuation range of $102M (low) to $138M (high), with a median of $120M. The low-end estimate reflects conservative application of the multiplier, while the high-end assumes stronger execution momentum within the Growth phase.

Key uncertainties stem from the sparse comparables universe and reliance on stage-progression alone. Without public market data or multiple private transactions to validate sector revenue multiples or growth-adjusted discount rates, this analysis carries elevated calibration risk. The valuation is most reliable as a baseline reflecting stage and capital deployed, but lacks corroboration from market-based or revenue-multiple approaches. Additional data on Ocean Infinity's revenue run rate, customer concentration, and capital efficiency metrics would materially improve precision.
